allergies: Nickel (contact dermatitis from some tool handles/buckles)
health.mental_health: Since the thyroid diagnosis last year, Taylor has been managing fatigue and mood swings; they’re functional at work but privately anxious about long-term stamina and feel embarrassed needing accommodations on site.
recent_visits: Endocrinology follow-up at Ito Hospital (thyroid labs and dose adjustment) - last month
Gastroenterology consult at a local clinic in Koto City (reflux flare and medication review) - 2 months ago
Annual health check through work (blood pressure and cholesterol screening) - 3 months ago
Pharmacy consultation about timing levothyroxine vs. antacids - recent

finance.debt: Carried over about ¥1.2M on a credit card and a small personal loan after several clinic visits, lab fees, and taking unpaid days during the worst fatigue period; currently on a repayment plan.
investments: NISA index funds (domestic and global equity)
Small cash emergency fund in a savings account
recent_transactions: ¥32,000 purchase of a home blood pressure monitor and reflux-friendly wedge pillow
¥14,500 reduced overtime payout (missed a weekend shift due to symptoms)

relationships: Partner: Mika Sato (supports medication routines and meal planning; occasional tension about Taylor working overtime despite fatigue)
Older sister: Vivian Wu (lives in Yokohama; checks in about health and finances; sometimes overbearing)
Close coworker/friend: Kenji Nakamura (covers for breaks on site; one of the few at work who knows about the thyroid issue)
Primary care contact: Dr. Harada (clinic physician; Taylor prefers direct, no-nonsense communication)
social.social_media_activity: Mostly lurks on X and Instagram; posts occasional photos of neighborhood walks and ramen shops but avoids personal updates. Uses a private LINE group with a few friends and a separate LINE thread with partner for health reminders.
private_conversations: Whether to disclose the thyroid diagnosis to HR and the union rep to request reduced night work
Concerns about fertility/sexual side effects and body changes after starting levothyroxine
Fear of being seen as unreliable on site and losing promotion opportunities
Arguments with partner about diet changes (less spicy/fatty foods) and alcohol reduction

schedule.work_hours: Typically 06:30–17:30 on weekdays; rotating Saturdays during peak phases; occasional night pours (1–2 times/month) but trying to reduce due to health.
regular_appointments: Monthly thyroid blood test and endocrinology follow-up (weekday morning)
Quarterly blood pressure check at the clinic/pharmacy
Biweekly refill pickup at the pharmacy near Monzen-Nakacho
Weekly meal prep planning with partner (Sunday evening) to manage reflux triggers
upcoming_events: Endocrinology appointment for dose review next Tuesday 09:00
Safety training refresher and toolbox talk presentation next Friday 07:00
Site inspection with client rep in Shinagawa next month
Weekend visit to sister in Yokohama planned (pending energy levels)
schedule.daily_routine: Up at 05:15, levothyroxine on an empty stomach, commute by train, morning site briefing, staggered breaks to manage reflux, tries to eat smaller lunches, home by early evening, short walk when energy allows, early bedtime; avoids late-night meals to reduce symptoms.

professional.company: Mid-sized general contractor based in Tokyo (Shinagawa office) specializing in commercial fit-outs and mid-rise concrete work
projects: Office renovation and seismic retrofit in Minato City
Mixed-use mid-rise foundation and rebar work in Shinagawa
Warehouse roof replacement and drainage upgrade in Ota Ward
professional.performance_issues: Over the past year, fatigue and reflux flares have caused a few late arrivals and one missed night pour; Taylor has been quietly delegating more physical tasks and relying on a senior lead to cover, worried it may affect their reputation.
workplace_relationships: Direct supervisor: Hiroshi Tanabe (values output; skeptical of health-related schedule changes)
Site safety officer: Ayumi Kato (supportive; encourages breaks and hydration, discreet about accommodations)
Crew dynamics: generally respectful but some older subcontractors make occasional comments about Taylor’s gender presentation; Taylor avoids escalation to keep the site running

hobbies: Early-morning neighborhood walks along the Sumida River
Building and painting small-scale model kits (Gundam/plamo)
Visiting sento (public baths) for muscle recovery on lighter weeks
Watching baseball (Tokyo Dome games when schedule allows)
habits: Tracks thyroid symptoms and blood pressure in a phone notes app
Keeps antacids and a water bottle in their work bag at all times
Avoids late-night meals; elevates head during sleep to prevent reflux
Limits alcohol to a couple of drinks on rare social occasions (reduced significantly after diagnosis)
Uses nicotine gum occasionally during high-stress periods to avoid returning to smoking
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives with partner in a rented 2LDK apartment in Koto City; chose it for a shorter commute and quieter evenings to support sleep and symptom management.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Leans toward reflux-friendly meals: smaller portions, less fried food, less chili/garlic, more grilled fish, tofu, rice, and vegetables; still craves spicy ramen but treats it as an occasional, carefully timed indulgence.
